title: Offices of Real Estate Agents and Brokers

narrative.executive_view: A residential brokerage can automate transaction coordination, listing preparation, marketing, bookkeeping and first-pass pricing work, but it is a weak pure AI-labor roll-up. The supplied wage base is only 18.26% of receipts and the largest economic outflow is commission paid to independent-contractor agents, not employee labor. Compass paid 81.1% of stand-alone brokerage revenue as commissions and related expense in early 2026 [S6], and Real's standard model lets agents retain 85% until a cap and 100% thereafter [S7]. AI can make agents more productive without creating much acquirer-owned margin. Buyability exists, but durable value depends on recruiting, lead flow and ancillary services rather than office-cost removal.
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: NAR's 2025 survey found 41% of REALTORS using AI or generative AI, with 20% using AI daily and 22% weekly [S2]. Listing copy, comparative market analysis, marketing assets, scheduling, form preparation and transaction coordination are already exposed. The human remainder is pricing judgment under local conditions, negotiation, trust, exception handling and licensed accountability. The supplied 3-digit occupation mix is badly contaminated by lessors and property managers, so maintenance, janitorial and property-management roles receive bounded task judgments but are not presented as the actual structure of a 531210 brokerage.
narrative.value_capture: Most automation value initially accrues to the individual agent or upstream platform rather than the acquired brokerage. Compass describes its agents as independent contractors and paid $5.68 billion of $6.96 billion 2025 revenue as commissions and related expense [S6]; Real explicitly offers an 85/15 split followed by 100% to the agent after the annual cap [S7]. A brokerage can retain savings from employee transaction coordinators, office support and management, but those are a small base. It also faces competition through higher splits and recruiting incentives. The NAR settlement made compensation more explicit and negotiable [S4], though observed buyer-agent rates have so far remained sticky [S5].
narrative.adoption_timing: Tool use is already close to the halfway threshold: 41% reported using AI or generative AI, while 42% used it daily or weekly [S2]. That is individual-agent usage, not verified brokerage-wide labor replacement, so current effective adoption is set at 41% with a caveat and time to 50% at 1.5 years. Adoption is likely to be fast because agents buy consumer tools directly, yet operator-level integration into compliance, transaction and accounting systems will lag. The main gating factors are brokerage policy, data quality, fair-housing risk, MLS permissions and whether tools become reliable enough to remove staff rather than merely accelerate drafts.
narrative.consolidation_economics: There is visible consolidation but no insurance-style deep roll-up market. Compass completed Anywhere and had already acquired other brokerages [S6]; Rocket completed Redfin in 2025 [S10]; Real announced an $880 million RE/MAX transaction in 2026 [S9]. For entry, Axial reports a recently closed $2.0 million EBITDA real-estate-broker transaction at 4.01x, inside the target band [S8]. For exit, the Real-RE/MAX filing states 7x fully synergized 2025 EBITDA, while the transaction also includes a global franchise system and mortgage brand [S9]. I use 4.0x buy and 7.0x exit as transparent estimates, with a very wide range because business mix, agent retention, brand and company-dollar basis dominate value.
narrative.terminal_demand: Full-commission intermediation is pressured, not yet melting. California requires a license before acting as a broker or salesperson [S11], and consumers still value local judgment and negotiation in a high-stakes transaction. But licenses do not require consumers to buy a traditional full-service package, and AI plus portals can unbundle discovery, pricing and paperwork. Post-settlement data show buyer-agent commissions rebounded to roughly pre-change levels in 2025, including 2.52% below $500,000 and 2.34% for $500,000–$999,999 homes [S5]. That supports near-term durability, while explicit negotiation and low-fee licensed wrappers remain credible long-term pressure.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The central measurement problem is scope. Census defines 531210 as agents and brokers selling, buying or renting real estate for others [S1], but the deterministic OEWS mix is 3-digit 531 and contains large blocks of maintenance, property management, janitorial and rental-clerk labor from sibling codes. NAR sources cover members rather than brokerage owners, Axial's real-estate-broker category may not perfectly isolate residential 531210, and public transactions include franchising, title, mortgage or portal assets. Gross commission income, company dollar and EBITDA must never be mixed. A buyer must underwrite agent retention, split resets, owner production, lead ownership, franchise royalty obligations and ancillary-service earnings separately.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS Definition — 531210 Offices of Real Estate Agents and Brokers
sources.what_it_supports: Exact six-digit scope: establishments acting as agents or brokers in selling, buying or renting real estate for others.

sources.title: 2025 REALTORS Technology Survey
sources.what_it_supports: REALTOR technology and AI usage: 41% using AI or generative AI, 20% daily, 22% weekly, and task-adoption context.

sources.title: Income Steady, Even as Market Slows: 2025 Member Trends
sources.what_it_supports: NAR member demographics: median age 57 and 44% over age 60, plus the continuing role of repeat and referral business.

sources.title: NAR Practice Change Implementation on August 17, 2024
sources.what_it_supports: Offers of buyer-broker compensation were removed from MLSs and written buyer agreements became required before touring.

sources.title: Buyer's Agent Commissions Tick Up to Pre-NAR Settlement Levels
sources.what_it_supports: Q2 2025 buyer-agent commission rates by home-price tier and evidence that rates had rebounded despite greater negotiation.

sources.title: Compass 2025 Annual Report on Form 10-K
sources.what_it_supports: Agent commissions as independent-contractor pass-through expense, 2025 revenue and commission expense, and completion of the Anywhere acquisition.

sources.title: The Real Brokerage 2025 Annual Information Form
sources.what_it_supports: Real's 85/15 agent split, annual cap and 100% agent retention after the cap, illustrating thin retained company dollar.

sources.title: Real Estate Brokers M&A Activity and Market Trends
sources.what_it_supports: Recent lower-middle-market closed transactions, including a $2.0M EBITDA deal at 4.01x and a broad 2.5x–10.8x observed range.

sources.title: Real to Acquire REMAX — SEC Exhibit 99.1
sources.what_it_supports: Announced $880M acquisition at 7x fully synergized 2025 EBITDA and consolidation led by an AI-enabled brokerage platform.

sources.title: Rocket Companies 2025 Annual Report on Form 10-K
sources.what_it_supports: Rocket's completed 2025 acquisition of Redfin, confirming another active strategic consolidator and the value of portal, brokerage and mortgage integration.

sources.title: California Department of Real Estate Program Areas — Licensing
sources.what_it_supports: A person must obtain a state license before acting or advertising as a real estate broker or salesperson in California.

labor_share.method: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 531210 ($30,836,359,913) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 531210 ($180,013,344,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

occupations.title: Property, Real Estate, and Community Association Managers
Janitors and Cleaners, Except Maids and Housekeeping Cleaners
Secretaries and Administrative Assistants, Except Legal, Medical, and Executive
First-Line Supervisors of Mechanics, Installers, and Repairers

n_band.derivation: margin 0.0396 (margins.json[53]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Real Estate (Operations & Services) EBITDA/Sales 3.96% (agency/brokerage/property-management economics, dominant in firm counts)); revenue/employee $432,174 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 531210); class <5: 140,557 firms, avg revenue/firm $449,562 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $17,803 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 6,638 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,862,814 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $113,367 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 2,095 firms, avg revenue/firm $6,465,620 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $256,039 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 1,204 firms, avg revenue/firm $16,484,213 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $652,775 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 259 firms, avg revenue/firm $48,286,429 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,912,143 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 139 firms, avg revenue/firm $373,541,367 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $14,792,238 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 259
n_band.margin_source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Real Estate (Operations & Services) EBITDA/Sales 3.96% (agency/brokerage/property-management economics, dominant in firm counts)

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: Fractions reflect task automation evidenced by rapid agent AI use and digital brokerage operations, but every deterministic SOC is retained exactly even though the 3-digit mix is materially contaminated by lessor and property-management work. Physical roles are assigned low fractions; brokerage administration and agent preparation receive higher fractions.
ai_replaceable_share.plausible_range: 0.28–0.50 of the supplied proxy wage mix
caveats: The 3-digit 531 role mix is not representative of exact 531210; maintenance, janitorial, rental and property-management roles largely belong to sibling codes.
Most real estate agents are independent contractors, so automation of agent tasks is not equivalent to reducing the supplied W-2 wage base or improving brokerage EBITDA.

breakdown_by_role.rationale: Diagnosis, dispatch preparation and documentation automate, but physical maintenance is not replaceable by current AI and is mostly proxy contamination.

breakdown_by_role.role: Property, Real Estate, and Community Association Managers
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Tenant communication, reporting and workflow coordination automate, while field oversight, compliance and dispute handling remain; the role is mostly outside 531210.
Listing preparation, CMA drafts, marketing, scheduling and paperwork automate; licensed judgment, negotiation, trust and local execution remain.
Availability, quoting, standard contracts and routine inquiries automate, while customer exceptions and physical handoff remain; largely sibling-code contamination.
Listing entry, scheduling, document routing and routine correspondence are strong automation candidates.
Reporting, recruiting analytics and routine operations automate, while supervision, agent retention, legal accountability and strategy remain.

breakdown_by_role.role: Janitors and Cleaners, Except Maids and Housekeeping Cleaners
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Scheduling can automate but cleaning remains physical and is almost entirely a sibling-code proxy role.

breakdown_by_role.role: Secretaries and Administrative Assistants, Except Legal, Medical, and Executive
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Calendar, form, correspondence and coordination work are mature AI and workflow-automation targets.
Commission calculation, reconciliation, posting and standard reporting automate; exception review and controls remain.

breakdown_by_role.role: First-Line Supervisors of Mechanics, Installers, and Repairers
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Work-order triage and reporting automate, but physical supervision and safety judgment persist; the role is proxy contamination.
The unlisted tail likely mixes actual brokerage marketing and transaction support with appraisal, leasing, IT and other 531 occupations; a middle fraction reflects that uncertainty.

pi_dist.rationale: A brokerage can retain savings on its own employee overhead, but most gross commission is contractually paid to independent agents and competition reallocates economics through splits, caps and recruiting incentives. More explicit commission negotiation creates an additional pass-through channel to consumers.
caveats: Compass and Real are large public models, not a direct measure of every private brokerage; savings from automating 1099 agent work are not operator labor savings.

pi_moat.rationale: Local brand, repeat business and operational support provide some retention, but agents and their books are portable, leading platforms compete on splits and technology, and portals or mortgage ecosystems control valuable lead flow. Licensing is reserved for terminal-demand analysis rather than counted as capture moat.
caveats: A dominant local brand with company-owned leads can be materially stronger; an owner-dependent or agent-portable book can be weaker.

t50_years.rationale: Reported AI use is already 41%, and 42% use it daily or weekly. Consumer tools can cross 50% quickly, but moving from individual drafting to effective operator workflows justifies more than a few months.
caveats: NAR measures individual REALTOR use, not verified brokerage process coverage or labor replacement.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: NAR's 2025 random-sample survey reported 41% of REALTORS currently using AI or generative AI.
current_adoption_pct.plausible_range: 0.41 observed tool use; effective integrated workflow use is likely lower
caveats: The survey had a 2.5% response rate and is member-level rather than firm-level; tool use is a broad proxy for effective adoption.

historical_analogs.value: MLS, e-signature, electronic forms, CRM and portal adoption made agents digitally productive without eliminating brokerage or transferring most agent economics to the brokerage; AI is likely to extend that pattern before deeper unbundling occurs.
historical_analogs.rationale: NAR reports very high use of e-signature and broad AI uptake, while public brokerage filings still show agent-centered commission economics. The analog supports fast task adoption but weak operator capture.
historical_analogs.plausible_range: 1–3 years for majority tool use; longer for structural business-model change
caveats: Generative AI may unbundle advice and discovery more deeply than prior workflow tools, so the analog may understate terminal pressure.

owners_60plus_pct.rationale: NAR reports 44% of members over 60. It is used as a bounded proxy for broker-owner age because a current, owner-specific national percentage was not found.
caveats: NAR members include sales agents, associate brokers and nonowners; member age cannot be treated as direct owner age.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: An aging member population is documented, but the source does not establish a shortage of qualified brokerage successors. The input is therefore conservatively false.
succession_shortage_documented.plausible_range: false; could be true for specific owner-dependent firms
caveats: Firm-level succession planning must be diligenced; member demographics alone do not prove a channel-wide shortage.

active_consolidators.rationale: Three distinct strategic acquirers are directly documented in recent filings: Compass acquired Anywhere, Rocket acquired Redfin and Real announced the REMAX acquisition.
active_consolidators.plausible_range: 3 directly documented large strategics; additional regional buyers exist
caveats: These are large strategic transactions, not proof of a deep, repeatable buyer pool for independent $1–10M EBITDA brokerages.

buy_mult.rationale: Axial reports a recent $2.0M EBITDA real-estate-broker transaction at 4.01x, directly inside the target band. The selected value rounds that comp and sits near the middle of smaller reported deals.
caveats: Axial's category may include business mixes beyond exact residential 531210; the single comp is anonymized, and EBITDA must be normalized after owner production and agent splits.

exit_mult.rationale: The 7x fully synergized Real-REMAX transaction provides a current scaled-platform anchor above the 4x target comp. A successful regional platform is estimated at 7x without assuming the 9.4x pre-synergy RE/MAX figure.
caveats: REMAX includes global franchise and mortgage assets, brand value and $30M expected synergies; it is not a clean independent-brokerage comp, and Axial's observed range is extremely wide.

pricing_structure.value: Transaction-based percentage commission with most gross commission passed to independent-contractor agents; brokerage retains company dollar, fees and sometimes franchise or ancillary revenue
pricing_structure.rationale: NAR documents negotiable buyer agreements, Redfin measures percentage commissions, and public filings document independent-agent pass-through and capped split structures.
pricing_structure.plausible_range: Brokerage retention commonly a minority of gross commission and highly model-dependent
caveats: Owned brokerage, franchise, cap, desk-fee, team and ancillary-service models have different revenue bases; compare valuations only on normalized retained EBITDA.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: The replaceability estimate applies to mature task capability, while 41% current tool use and 1.5 years to majority adoption imply a near-term but partial operating ramp; neither converts 1099 agent effort into operator wage savings.

terminal_value.justification: Licensed execution, negotiation and trust preserve paid demand, but portals and AI can unbundle discovery, pricing and paperwork, and explicit commission negotiation can compress full-service percentage fees toward lower-cost licensed wrappers.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: Exact 531210 scope is anchored to Census. The 3-digit occupation mix, NAR member demographics, Axial deal category and public platform transactions are disclosed proxies; property management, lessors, appraisal, title and mortgage economics are not silently imported.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Observed buyer-agent rates were sticky through Q2 2025, but settlement rules increased explicit negotiation. AI can lower the cost of service and enable flat-fee or low-fee models even if near-term percentage rates remain resilient.

dataset_mismatch.description: The supplied role mix is 3-digit NAICS 531, not exact 531210, and is heavily contaminated by lessors and property managers; agent commissions paid to 1099 workers are also outside the W-2 wage base.
sensitivity: Removing maintenance, janitorial, rental and property-management occupations would materially change the role-weighted replaceable share.
A true 531210 mix would place more weight on agents, transaction coordination, marketing, brokerage management and commission accounting.
Even a higher task-replaceable share may produce little operator value if the affected work is performed by independent contractors rather than employees.

market_boundary.dominant_subsegment: Residential brokerage offices and regional franchisees
market_boundary.selection_basis: Residential brokerage dominates available member surveys, public filings and commission evidence. Property managers, lessors, appraisers, mortgage brokers and title businesses are excluded except where a public transaction's mixed perimeter is explicitly caveated.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: The adoption factor is high if current agent tool use becomes integrated workflow use quickly, but falls if policy and data constraints stall deployment.
The contaminated 3-digit role mix can move V materially and is the largest mechanical weakness in the record.
Owner dependence, agent retention and revenue-basis normalization can erase the apparent discount.
Platform quality and ancillary assets determine whether any multiple expansion is achievable.
If agents or consumers capture most productivity gains, the operator's already-small AI value pool shrinks further.

reviewer_note: This record explicitly rejects the structural error of treating 1099 agent splits as automatable employee labor. Exact six-digit scope and both buy/exit multiples are sourced, with the Axial and public-platform multiple proxies clearly disclosed.
